name: result_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_result_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Alignment-down should never produce a value larger than the original input

name: unaligned_input_unchanged
code:
proof fn phi_2_unaligned_input_unchanged(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 1,
        a % b != 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == a,
{
}
reason: If an unaligned value is returned unchanged, the function fails its core purpose of aligning down

name: result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of align_to_usize must always be a multiple of b; if not, the alignment is broken

name: different_granularity_equal
code:
proof fn phi_4_different_granularity_equal(a: usize)
    requires
        a < 0x1_0000_0000usize,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, L1_ENTRY_SIZE) == align_to_usize(a, L2_ENTRY_SIZE),
{
}
reason: Coarser and finer alignment granularities collapsing to the same value would indicate the spec conflates page sizes

name: gap_exceeds_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_5_gap_exceeds_alignment(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        sub(a, align_to_usize(a, b)) >= b,
{
}
reason: The rounding-down gap must always be strictly less than b; a gap >= b means the result skipped past a valid aligned address
